channel.title: Convergence Daily
channel.description: Real Convergence Daily episodes published through the dual-native engine.

item.title: All the Bottlenecks, None of the Asymmetry
item.description: AI infrastructure is the loudest thesis in public markets and the corpus confirms the underlying bottleneck dynamics are real — HBM and advanced packaging constraint, China export-control compounding risk, and hyperscaler capex pull-through visible in secondary-layer vendor IR — but the options scanner shows peak-hype convergence at the headline names and no clean asymmetric expression in the secondary bottleneck-stack names where the information edge actually lives.

item.title: The Clocks Are Not Running at the Same Speed
item.description: Bitcoin mining has split into two structurally distinct businesses with different risk clocks — pure-play hashprice names (MARA, RIOT, CLSK) cooling post-peak and AI/HPC infrastructure platforms (CORZ, HUT, IREN, BTBT, HIVE) building lease duration quietly — while the corpus carries 0.80–0.83 confidence on three independent claims and the decision packet sits at watchlist_candidate awaiting a confirming PPA or earnings catalyst before equity entry is warranted.

item.title: When the Shortage List Gets Resolved
item.description: DTC telehealth platforms have built material compounded GLP-1 revenues explicitly contingent on FDA shortage-list status — a binary regulatory event that collapses this revenue with no appeal mechanism.

item.title: Five Hundred Patients Before the Market Noticed
item.description: CRISPR Therapeutics has cleared the first commercial-compounding threshold in public gene editing — $43M Q1 2026 Casgevy revenue, 500-plus patient initiations, $2.44B cash — while the scanner returns one passing contract across the entire synthetic biology theme and equities have not re-rated from clinical-stage to commercial-compounder multiples.

item.title: The Factory Behind the Drug Nobody Can Get
item.description: Peptide API manufacturing capacity is the structural constraint on GLP-1 supply — observable in primary CDMO investor filings at 0.80 corpus confidence — while originator names trade at peak-hype convergence and the manufacturing layer sits entirely outside option flow.

item.title: The Gearbox Between Demo and Revenue
item.description: Humanoid robotics has crossed from demo to commercial pilot — Tesla/Optimus in quarterly IR, Figure/BMW under formal agreement, Apptronik at Mercedes-Benz — while the options market prices TSLA at MEDIUM/growing convergence (0.50) with no isolation of the actuator supply constraint that governs cost-per-unit and deployment velocity.

item.title: The Market Is Long the Brand, Short the Molecule
item.description: The GLP-1 obesity trade is fully priced at the originator level — LLY and NVO sit at peak-hype convergence with elevated IV and deep-OTM options expressing a narrative the market already knows.

item.title: The Optical Layer Running Ahead of the Tape
item.description: Photonic computing has cleared three simultaneous conditions for a quiet setup: NVIDIA-anchored revenue inflection at 0.88 corpus confidence, private chiplet validation at multi-billion-dollar marks, and zero passing options contracts on LITE or COHR.

item.title: Revenue Is Moving in the Dark
item.description: Photonic computing has crossed a confirmed revenue inflection — Lumentum +90% YoY, Coherent +21% YoY, NVIDIA $4B structural anchor — while the scanner records zero options contracts on both primary names and the convergence scorer still classifies the theme as emerging; the same AI-capex supercycle is visible in nuclear-SMR and defense-AI, but options premiums in those sectors have already partially taxed the expression.

item.title: The Light Nobody Is Trading
item.description: Silicon photonics is generating the real revenue inflection inside AI infrastructure while options flow concentrates in peak-hype names one layer up the stack.

item.title: The Bandwidth Wall Is Already Photons
item.description: Optical interconnect has crossed from research to production revenue inside the hyperscaler AI buildout, anchored by NVIDIA's March 2026 $4B dual commitment to Lumentum and Coherent, yet the options scanner finds zero qualifying structures for either name while private chiplet marks at Lightmatter ($4.4B) and Ayar Labs ($3.75B) signal the same 12-to-24-month private-leads-public revaluation pattern visible across five other sectors in this corpus.
